thig6.2:1.1: Overwhelmed with grief for my son—
thig6.2:1.3: naked, demented, my hair disheveled my mind deranged—
thig6.2:1.4: I went about here & there, living along the side of the road,
thig6.2:3.1: Then I saw the One Well-Gone,
thig6.2:3.2: gone to the city of Mithilā:
thig6.2:3.4: Self-Awakened, with nothing to fear from anything, anywhere.
thig6.2:4.2:
paying him homage, I sat myself down.
He, Gotama, from sympathy
thig6.2:5.3: Applying myself to the Teacher’s words,
thig6.2:5.4: I realized the state of auspicious bliss.
thig6.2:6.1: All griefs have been cut off,
thig6.2:6.3: for I’ve comprehended the grounds from which griefs
